In Parliament

  • Tweet

You can view my recent speeches and appearances in Parliament below:

Engagements | Prime Minister | Commons debates

Many of us across this House worked hard to pass the Online Safety Act 2023, not because we thought it would be the last word on online safe

Engagements | Prime Minister | Commons debates

Many of us across this House worked hard to pass the Online Safety Act 2023, not because we thought it would be the last word on online safe

Elections: Campaigns | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

The Electoral Commission has not made such an assessment. There are no requirements in law on the language of imprints.

Polling Stations: Religious Buildings | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

Individual local authorities are responsible for designating polling places.

Polling Stations: Religious Buildings | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

The Commission provides guidance to Returning Officers on identifying suitable polling station venues.

Elections | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

The Electoral Commission does not have a memorandum of understanding with the Metropolitan Police.

Members: Finance | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

The Electoral Commission has a memorandum of understanding with the Parliamentary Commissioner for Standards, which outlines how the two rep

Political Parties: Finance | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers

Commercial transactions may be sponsorship, if the purpose of the payment can reasonably be seen to be to help the members association meet

Lord Mandelson Humble Address: Government Response Update | Commons debates

Forgive me, Madam Deputy Speaker.

Lord Mandelson Humble Address: Government Response Update | Commons debates

I thank the Minister for his kind words about the hard work of the Intelligence and Security Committee.
